replies.author: @OtterloopB
replies.text:
Disney ruined movies as a whole for me. When Episode VII came out and I watched Han Solo die on that bridge... something in me broke and any passion or excitement I had for movies was just... _gone._ 

I've tried multiple times to get back into film since 2015. My first attempt was by watching Incredibles 2 in 2019... and that was *awful.* I then tried Coco... it was better, but nothing in me fell back in love with movies because of it.

Ghostbusters: Afterlife I thought was great, and Mario was fun... but I've accepted that, for me, movies no longer mean anything special. I'll stick to games and books for entertainment. I jumped off the Titanic that is now Cinema 8 years ago...

author: @spacedave2000
text: This is a generational battle.  One we allowed  to happen.  The Millenials now rule the roost and have gone through all the programming of high school and college.  They think they are more moral and righteous then our generation and the Boomer generation.  That we are all racist, and phobs.  We've allowed them to berate us, demean us and criticize us and our historical franchises and characters.  They've 'punished' us through taking those beloved characters and bending them and breaking them down into how they see us.  So it's no surprise, we having the values we do...but also the money we do...not going to see their derived mess.  Then...the GenZ kids don't care about any of it and have no money either...so they don't go see it.  The Millenials and GenZ are more then happy to placate to communism...yet capitalism has spoken loud and clear "GET WOKE GO BROKE!"

replies.author: @robintst
replies.text: It's more the younger half of Millennials, the 90s babies. replies.text:
Even this channel, which in itself should be classified as a hate group by the FBI, there is no place for the kind of vile words of assault you used.
Just accept that us millennials are in charge now.
There is no more American flag, only a rainbow one. 
Remember that and we’ll let you keep living here.
Once we get an even more free version of Roe vs. Wade passed, next we come for euthanasia.
The old people are next. 
There will be no more nursing homes, therefore a better version of Socialism will prevail because he won’t have to pay for old people in homes.
It may be hard to hear, but it is for a better future for all.

replies.author: @andywellsglobaldomination
replies.text: I respectfully disagree.  The people in charge are not millenials.  Kathleen Kenndy is not a millenial.   12st century people are different from the rest of humanity because they never knew a world without smart phones, tablets and social media.  Disney's sin is trying to just retrtofit Boomer content for Millenials.  They're the ones being cheated, because they deserve their own heroes, not debased versions of Boomer heroes. Lucas films was never just a list of IPs, they reflected Lucas' vision of what made a good movie... and were soundly based on his life as a movie lover.  The vision was not for sale; all Disney got were the remains.

replies.author: @StephenGillie
replies.text: It's a generational issue, but not a Boomer vs Millennial battle. instead it's Boomer retirement, taking their money from risky stocks and putting into much safer Treasury bills. Part of this is investor math, where you invest in 10 companies, one makes 20x profits, and the other 9 are basically charities. "Ironically", the midpoint is when the interest rate on those bills went from 0% to 5%. So all of the money is leaving the world, going into the "can you retire on $500k" ads. This means that 9/10 businesses are being forced to take a crash course on giving customers what we want, and a few are failing badly in public. You're right that GenZ have relatively no money, and most Millennials are still young and liberal. The lack of money will fix some of that, and time will fix more of it - this is the Great Unraveling in action.

replies.author: @spacedave2000
replies.text:
@StephenGillie  I'm referring more to boomers and GenX (my generation) vs millennial and GenZ.  There is some odd disconnect between those.  The Boomers rely on their GenX kids to explain it all...and we are realizing that although communism lost the cold war it quickly transitioned into public schools in North America and Europe/UK right under our noses.  They have been rewriting history, economic values, moral values, spirituality, ethics, etc right in front of us.  
Objectively I think Indiana Jones is a hard sell to a new generation who doesn't have an imagination for the 20s and 30s or WW2.  That's literally 100 years ago.  But they sure do think slavery was just abolished a couple years ago.  Such an odd generation!

I understand Disney wanting this franchise to live longer than one movie and MAYBE even be something they could make content for on D+, though I think that'd be a mistake especially seeing what Young Indiana Jones did on TVs as a kid... though the heavy handed messaging must be attractive to current Disney.
But there were SO MANY ways they could have made this film that didn't even put Harrison Ford in harms way yet still told a compelling story.  They are bankrupt in the only way that matters to their industry... creativity.

replies.author: @samhiltz1107
replies.text:
They know what they're doing. And the do care - they're trying to corner the market on "Woke" programming, believing that it's going to be short-term pain for long term gain.

Think of it like a very fat person trying to lose weight - there comes a point where it is nothing but pain, there is no apparent gain to be made, and it would make everyone feel better if they quit. But if the push through that pain, they will emerge on the other side a better person. That's what Disney is thinking right now- push through this pain and emerge better.

Unfortunately...they're dragging all of us through this process and we don't think Disney is going to be better at the end.

author: @christianale
text:
Hey, I got till the end as you mentioned, and yes, you got a new subscriber... 🎉
Didn't know you, just saw you for the first time yesterday on VR's Stream with WDW and the rest of the guys. Awesome content as usual. 
I've been tracking Box Office since 1987, been a Variety (weekly, on paper waaaay before internet...) subscriber for almost 10 years, so I love numbers, and yes, I agree with you, there's no way in hell this movie spent only 100m on P&A, an absolute joke.
I think this movie will be a turning point, we are not done yet, we have to keep fighting back, but we are going to win.
I know Disney can afford to lose money cos "the message" is been delivered, so their friends at WEF or Blackrock will keep pumping money on them, but sooner rather than later, even that won't matter.
Keep the good stuff, my dude

author: @stanley13579
text: CULTURE!! I keep forgetting to subscribe to you! I always see you on WDW Pro, and M.E., but I'm always so entertained by the end, I never think to seek you out! It's an absolute hoot to watch mainstream Hollywood implode the way it has. Fingers crossed that it will get better within the next 70 years; I hear Jenifer Lawrence will be the first woman to invent a pill which lets us live well into our hundreds, but only so long as we consume films shot in Los Angeles, Atlanta, British Columbia, Toronto, or Boston (but not Houston, because the craft services in Texas would probably kill everybody).

author: @DawgofAges
text: Iger thinks he is the MLK of the 'modern audience'.  What he fails to recognize is that the 'modern audience' is the same as the old audience.  The 'modern audience' is a few enabled loud voices that are being heeded by corporate leaders who do so to avoid criticism.  This was vogue a few years ago and now the correction has finally begun.  Average Americans are the same today as they were when Walt devised the Disney empire.  Families still want to love Disney.  That gives them a leg up on Bud Light, Target, etc because there is no built in family connections with those brands.  Disney has the easiest path back into the hearts of Americans.  Start today by re-instating "Ladies and Gentlemen, boys and girls" before the shows, re-distribute classic Disney movies, can the wokies and start a new advertising campaign, "Return of the magic".  We'll forgive and eat it up.

author: @TheFly212
text:
My regal theaters in Denver are $17.50 per ticket for adults.....The AMC is slightly cheaper at 17 bucks. The IMAX...... 22 dollars for adult. 

Matinees are about 3 dollars cheaper give or take. Still.... A "budget" movie is still 14 bucks or so..... Yeah. A small coke at AMC theaters is 11 bucks. A small popcorn 12.50. Candy can go as low as 8 bukcs (dots, m&ms) to as high as 12 bucks (recees pieces, hersheys big bar, butter finger). 


Yeah. I mean, I just simply cannot afford to spend almost 30 bucks to see a movie (I mean, you're gonna at least want something to drink when you see a movie, if not popcorn.). If you get a drink (small BTW). And small popcorn at my local AMC that's 17 bucks for the ticket, 11 bucks for the 24 oz coke, and 12.50 for the popcorn. One person. That's what.... 40+ bucks to see a movie.....Matinees ain't that much cheaper either. They used to be. I remember a time when matinee tickets her almost half the price what an evening showing was... But not anymore. They're mayabe 20 percent cheaper. Maybe. 

so you want someone to spend 40 bucks to go to the movies.... Yeah. Okay. No wonder know one is going to theaters.

author: @philfedora495
text: Watched Dial of Destiny yesterday. Its not terrible but its not good either. The Phoebe Waller Bridge (PWB) character isnt particularly infuriating, she's actually very boring. She's not funny, she's not interesting, she;s not sympathetic, she should be but i dont think she can act that well. It just felt like Indy was looking after a hyperactive child in her, she gives the impression of being a brainless 5 year old. The film itself is incredibly mediocre, not much action, the stunts dont feel tense or thrilling, the effects are bargain basement. Madds Mikelson is a very generic villian, who's more incompetant than scary. The climax of the film is .....an anticlimax. No great effects or stunts. The film was not woke at all it was boring if anything. There were no amazing scenes like the ball in Raiders, or the Airship in Last Crusade, it felt very cheap, I dont know where the $300million went but it wasnt on the film. Its not a film that needed to be made and if it was going to be made it should have had someone like Chris Pratt instead of PWB as a potential successor Indy comes out of retirement to help. I'd give this film 5/10.
